title: Route paid reactivation through the card-collecting checkout (real MP preapproval)

tasks.title: Document current reactivate wiring, web callers, and webhook activation handler
tasks.description: Read-only investigation before touching code. Produce a short notes.md in the spec docs folder capturing file:line anchors used by later tasks. Tests: none (investigation).

tasks.title: Extract shared low-level paid-subscription-create helper
tasks.description: Create apps/api/src/services/billing/paid-subscription-create.ts. Extract the mode:'paid' create block from subscription-checkout.service.ts:657-674 (spec §6.2): subscriptions.create({ customerId, planId, priceId, mode:'paid', paymentMethodReturnUrl, notificationUrl, metadata }), resolve checkoutUrl = providerInitPoint ?? providerSandboxInitPoint, and throw SubscriptionCheckoutError('MISSING_INIT_POINT', ...) when absent (fail-closed, spec §12 reuses the existing error type). Helper takes a resolved planId + priceId (bridges planSlug vs planId mismatch, spec §6.2). RO-RO signature, JSDoc. Tests: unit tests in test/ — init point present -> returns { checkoutUrl, subscription }; absent -> throws MISSING_INIT_POINT; asserts create called with mode:'paid' + priceId + paymentMethodReturnUrl.

No behavior change to /start-paid — this is a pure extraction (spec R-5). tasks.title: Add plan-resolution + validation guard for reactivation
tasks.description: Implement the §6.1 server-side guard as a shared util (used by both reactivate methods). NOTE (T-001): resolve at RUNTIME via billing.plans.list() (like resolvePlanBySlug at subscription-checkout.service.ts:196-218), NOT the seed-time plans.config.ts. FIRST resolve the slug-vs-UUID ambiguity: reactivate's planId is passed straight to subscriptions.create({planId}); billing_plans.id is UUID, resolvePlanBySlug matches on plan.name (slug) — confirm which space reactivate's planId is before validating. Branches: unknown -> throw (fail-closed); free plan (plan.monthlyPriceArs === 0, e.g. TOURIST_FREE_PLAN) -> throw INVALID_REACTIVATION_PLAN; annual plan (no monthly price / annualPriceArs-only) -> throw ANNUAL_REACTIVATION_UNSUPPORTED (deferred to HOS-123). This is NEW code, no existing guard to extract. Add the two new error codes/types. Tests: unit tests for each branch (unknown / free / annual / valid monthly paid) asserting the exact error code and that no subscription is created.

subtasks.title: Resolve planId vs catalog; reject unknown
Unit tests for all four branches

tasks.title: Rewrite reactivateFromTrial to route through paid checkout
tasks.description: trial.service.ts reactivateFromTrial (~:840-847). Apply the T-004 guard first, ensure customer exists, resolve monthly priceId, call the T-002 helper -> real MP preapproval + checkoutUrl (fail-closed). Return { checkoutUrl, subscriptionId, status:'incomplete' } (spec §6.3). Write supersedesSubscriptionId into the new sub's metadata (keep convertedFromTrial marker). REMOVE the synchronous cancel-old-sub + audit + clearEntitlementCache (moved to webhook, T-007) so the trialing sub keeps granting entitlements during the MP window (spec §6.4/§6.5). Tests: unit test asserting create called with mode:'paid', returns checkoutUrl, sub is incomplete, old trialing sub NOT cancelled inline, supersedesSubscriptionId set.

subtasks.title: Call shared helper, return checkoutUrl + incomplete status

tasks.title: Rewrite reactivateSubscription to route through paid checkout
tasks.description: trial.service.ts reactivateSubscription (~:1031-1039). Same pattern as T-005: guard, ensure customer, resolve priceId, call T-002 helper, return { checkoutUrl, subscriptionId, status:'incomplete' }, write supersedesSubscriptionId (keep reactivatedFromCanceled marker), remove synchronous cancel/audit/clearEntitlementCache (deferred to webhook T-007). Tests: unit test mirroring T-005 for the lapsed-subscription reactivation case.

subtasks.title: Call shared helper, return checkoutUrl + incomplete status

tasks.title: Handle old-subscription supersession on webhook confirmation
tasks.description: CORRECTED anchors (T-001): the handler is processSubscriptionUpdated in apps/api/src/routes/webhooks/mercadopago/subscription-logic.ts (NOT routes/billing/), event subscription_preapproval.created/.updated (NOT subscription_authorized_payment.created). Insertion point: right after clearEntitlementCache at subscription-logic.ts:701. This function is GENERIC (handles paused/cancelled/past-due/active) — gate the new logic strictly on the PENDING_PROVIDER -> ACTIVE transition (previousStatus normalized incomplete->PENDING_PROVIDER at :414, mappedStatus ACTIVE). Read supersedesSubscriptionId from the confirmed sub's metadata and complete the swap atomically (spec §6.4): cancel the superseded sub, write the converted/reactivated audit row, and clearEntitlementCache — exactly once (spec AC-4). No-op when the marker is absent (normal /start-paid activations and every non-activation transition). Tests: integration test that a confirmed reactivation preapproval cancels the superseded sub + writes audit + clears cache once; a normal activation without the marker is unaffected; a non-activation transition (e.g. pause) never triggers the swap.

subtasks.title: Cancel superseded sub + audit + clearCache atomically, once

tasks.title: Update response schema + route handlers to return checkoutUrl
tasks.description: DECIDED (owner 2026-07-10): move request+response schemas to @repo/schemas (fix drift, single source of truth). Spec §7 contract change. CORRECTION (T-001): the response schemas are NOT in @repo/schemas today — they are LOCAL inline in apps/api/src/routes/billing/trial.ts (reactivateTrialResponseSchema :90-94, reactivateSubscriptionResponseSchema :465-470), both synchronous-success shapes with no checkoutUrl. @repo/schemas only has a dead unused ReactivateTrialRequestSchema. Recommended: introduce proper request+response schemas in @repo/schemas (checkoutUrl + status:'incomplete', mirror /start-paid) and wire the routes to them, fixing the drift — confirm this widening with owner. Update both route handlers (:349-353, :527-532) to return checkoutUrl + status. Tests: schema unit test for the new shape; route test asserting the paid reactivation response carries checkoutUrl.

tasks.description: Spec AC-8 / §12 / OQ-4. Run the detection query (status='active' AND mp_subscription_id IS NULL AND metadata markers convertedFromTrial/reactivatedFromCanceled) against staging and prod before merge. Zero rows (expected, endpoints unwired) -> record no-op in closeout.md. Rows present -> author a scripted, reversible remediation with owner sign-off. Tests: n/a (ops query); document result in close-out.

tasks.title: Harden webhook supersession cancel + map reactivate business errors to 4xx
tasks.description: From code review (MEDIUM + LOW). (a) In completeReactivationSupersession (subscription-logic.ts): distinguish a benign 'already cancelled' from a transient MP cancel failure by RE-FETCHING the superseded sub's status after the cancel attempt. Only write the audit row when the sub is confirmed cancelled (or already terminal). On a transient failure (still active/non-terminal after the attempt): Sentry-capture, log loudly, and DO NOT write the audit row (leave the pairing un-done so the T-016 reconcile backstop picks it up) — never falsely mark it done. (b) LOW: map the reactivate business-rule errors ('active subscription exists', 'no canceled subscription found') to proper 4xx (409 / 404) instead of 500 — add typed codes (e.g. ACTIVE_SUBSCRIPTION_EXISTS, NO_CANCELED_SUBSCRIPTION) to SubscriptionCheckoutErrorCode + the mapper, and throw them from the reactivate methods. Tests: transient-cancel-failure leaves no audit + Sentry called; already-cancelled writes audit once; the two business errors return 409/404.

subtasks.title: Re-fetch superseded status; audit only on confirmed cancel

tasks.title: Reconcile backstop cron for orphaned superseded subscriptions
tasks.description: Owner-decided (full robustness). New cron job apps/api/src/cron/jobs/reactivation-supersession-reconcile.job.ts mirroring the existing billing cron structure + registration. Finds superseded subscriptions that survived their supersessor's activation: a subscription S still in a non-terminal state (active/trialing) whose id appears in some ACTIVE subscription N's metadata.supersedesSubscriptionId, where the (N,S) supersession was never completed (no audit row). For each: cancel S (idempotent), write the reactivation audit row (same shape as the webhook), clearEntitlementCache if needed, Sentry-alert on anomalies. Idempotent + safe to run repeatedly. Tests: a matured orphan gets cancelled+audited once; a correctly-superseded (already cancelled) sub is left alone; a plain sub with no supersessor is untouched. Add the cron to the smoke/cron coverage.

subtasks.title: Create reconcile job mirroring existing cron structure
Query orphaned superseded subs (active supersessor, no audit)
